page: Microsoft Azure B32als_v2 is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the Basv2 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 32 virtual CPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, though it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark data shows strong performance in single-core CPU execution, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2702.69. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is also highly efficient, reaching 174.04 GB/sec. Memory subsystem performance is solid, highlighted by a strong PassMark memory score of 2924.1, alongside average memory bandwidth speeds of 116.36 GB/sec for large block reads. However, the instance exhibits weak performance in large-scale LLM inference, processing only 6.57 tokens per second for a 70B model prompt.

The B32als_v2 utilizes a burstable CPU allocation model, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads with variable CPU utilization. The low memory density of 2.0 GB per vCPU represents a resource tradeoff, prioritizing compute capacity over memory-intensive operations. Because there is no local storage, users must attach external premium storage, which introduces additional configuration considerations but allows for flexible storage scaling.

This server type is suited for workloads that experience periodic spikes in demand, such as build servers, code compilation, and low-to-medium traffic web serving. The 32 vCPUs provide ample parallel processing capacity for multi-threaded tasks like bzip3 decompression and small-scale LLM text generation, while the burstable model helps manage infrastructure costs during idle periods.
description: Microsoft Azure B32als_v2 is a burstable virtual machine from the Basv2 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. It features 32 vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage but supports premium storage options. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU performance and multi-threaded decompression capabilities, alongside average memory bandwidth. Due to its burstable CPU allocation and low memory density, this server offers a cost-efficient solution for workloads with variable CPU demands. It is well-suited for tasks such as software compilation, build pipelines, and web serving that require high parallel processing capacity during peak times without the continuous cost of dedicated CPU resources.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B32als_v2 is a burstable Basv2 virtual machine featuring 32 vCPUs, an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, and 64 GB of RAM. It is designed for cost-efficient, variable-demand workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure B32als_v2 is a burstable virtual machine with 32 vCPUs, an AMD EPYC 7763 CPU, and 64 GB RAM, optimized for variable workloads.
tagline: A burstable, low-memory virtual machine featuring thirty-two vCPUs for cost-effective handling of variable and spike-prone compute workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores.
Configured with 64.0 GB of RAM, offering a low-memory 2.0 GB per core ratio.
Utilizes a burstable CPU allocation model for cost-efficient resource scaling.
Lacks local storage but supports premium external storage attachments.
Delivers strong single-core CPU performance and multi-threaded decompression.
Best suited for software compilation, build pipelines, and variable web serving.
